s.m. A eunuch; hermaphrodite ( = saṇḍ or shaṇḍ).
adj. Amorously playful, elegant and sweet in manners (a mistress); elegant, beautiful, handsome; — s.m. A thief, robber.
more: 1. adj. Amorously playful, elegant and sweet in manners (a mistress). 2. s. A thief, robber, knave.

def: s.m. Cinnabar, vermilion, red sulphuret of mercury.
more: n. m. Cinnabar; vermilion; red sulphuret of mercury.

def: adj. Of the colour of vermilion, &c.
more: adj. Of the color of shaṅgraf .

ety: v.n. fr. شنع 'to be bad, or abominable,' &c.
def: adj. Bad, evil, foul, abominable, unseemly, shocking, base, disgraceful; adulterous.
more: (from شنع) adj. Disgraceful, shocking, abominable, base, adulterous.

ety: for A. شنيعة shanīʻat, fem. of shanīʻ
def: adj. = shanīʻ, q.v.; — s.f. ( = fěʻl-ě-shanīʻa), Badness, evil practice, abomination, turpitude; adultery; prostitution; sodomy.
more: H. n. m. 1. Adultery, 2. Prostitution. 3. Sodomy. fel-i-shanīa kī garaz se ujrat par chalānā . Ped. for /?/kharchī kamvānā . Letting to hire for the purpose of prostitution.

ety: v.n. of shanīdan; rt. Zend śru = S. श्रु
def: s.f. The act of hearing, listening.
more: (from شنيدن) s. f. Act of hearing.

ety: perf. part. of shanīdan; see shanīd
def: part. Heard; — s.m. That which is heard.
more: adj. Heard. Shanīdah kaë buad mānind-i-dīdāh . P. Prov. Hearing is not like seeing.

def: adj. Fit or worthy to be heard
more: adj. Fit or worthy to be heard.

def: s.m. = P. shangarf, q.v.
more: s. m. for Pers. شنگرف shangarf, q. v.

def: s. m. Name of a musical mode.
The followers of Shankarāchārj, q. v.
n. prop. Name of a Hindū faḳīr, celebrated for having eradicated the sect of jain.

ety: prob. fr. A. شنّة, inf. n. of un. fr. شنّ 'to scatter water,' &c
def: s.m. An inflated skin or leather bag (used in crossing a river), a raft, a float.
s. (dakh.) Art, cunning ( Harris ): see سيانپ syānap.

ety: Zend khshvas-daśan; S. षोडशन्
def: adj. Sixteen.

ety: -um = Zend aff. ma = S. म
def: adj. Sixteenth.

def: s.f. Sound or noise (of a whip, or spears, &c.), whiz; sound of splashing
more: n. f. Whiz; the noise made by a switch or whip. shap-shap, shapā-shap , adv. 1. Splash splash. 2. Quickly; fluently; without stopping. shapā-shap k. , v. a. 1. To splash; sputter; spatter. 2. To make a noise with the lips in lapping.
subs: adv. Splash, splash; — quickly, speedily; fluently; without cessation or stopping
v.n. To make a splashing noise, &c.; to splash; to clash; to sputter; — to make a noise with the lips in lapping, &c.

def: (dakh.) To clash ( Harris ).

ety: fr. S. क्षप् or क्षिप्
def: s.m. A butt or mark for archers.
more: s. m. A butt, or mark for archers.

r: shappar, shapar, shapparak, shaprak, shappara, shapara, shab, par, parīdan

r: shappara, shapara, shappar, shapar, shapparak, shaprak, shab, par, parīdan

r: shapparak, shaprak, shappar, shapar, shappara, shapara, shab, par, parīdan

more: s. (dakh.) A bat (the bird), flittermouse ( Harris ): see شپر shapar.

ety: = S. शारिः, or शारी
def: s.f. A starling; a mainā (q.v.).

ety: = S. शर
def: s.m. A fall of water (cf. āb-shār).

ety: inf. n. of شرع 'to be manifest or plain,' &c.
def: s.m. A high road; the divine way of religion, the precepts of Moḥammad, Moḥammadan law (as derived from the Qorʼān), law, equity
more: n. f. The law, or precepts of Mahomedanism as derived from the Qurān. Sharٴa meṅ kyā sharam hai . Prov. There is no shame in doing what the law allows. shara par chalnā , v. n. To act according to Mah. law. sharٴa ke khilāf k. , v. a. To act contrary to Mah. law. shara mahammadī , n. f. Mahomedan law. sharٴa meṅ rٴakhna ḍālnā, yā nikālnā , v. a. To make innovations in religion. be-shara , adj. Contrary to the Mah. law; unlaful.
s. m. 1. The precepts of Muḥammad, law, equity. 2. A high road.
subs: To walk or act according to the law
adj. Contrary to the law, unlawful; lawless

ety: for A. شري sharā
def: s.m. An eruption of pimples on the skin.

ety: v.n. fr. شرب 'to drink' (water, &c.
def: s.f. Wine, spirituous liquor
more: n. f. شرب drank. Spirits ( dārū, 1 ) Javānī aur us par sharāb, dūnī āg lagtī hai . Prov. Wine and youth as fire upon fire. sharāb-i-purtagālī , Poet. n. f. Port Wine. sharāb pīnā , v. a. To drink; liquor up. 1. Sharāb pī-kar bhūt ho gayā . Drunken with wine a demon made. 2. Woh sharāb pānī kī tarah pītā hai . Prov. Saying. He drinks wine like water (like a fish). sharāb-khāna , n. m. A tavern; public-house. sharāb-khuārī, sharāb-khorī, sharāb-noshī , n. f. Drinking; drunkenness; orgies. sharāb-i-do-ātshah . Double-distilled spirits. sharāb kheṅchne-vālā , n. m. A manufacturer of spirituous liquors; a distiller. sharāban tahūrā, sharāb-i-tahūr . Nectar. Zāhid, na tum pīo, na kisī ko pilā sako, Kyā bāt hai tumhārī sharāb-i-tahūr kī! Gālib. O man devout! you neither drink, nor serve to others out, Of what avail's this nectar that you preach so much about?

(from شرب To drink) s. f. Wine, spirituous liquor. شراب پرتگالي sharāb-i-purtugālī, Portugal wine. شرابا طہورا sharābaṉ t̤ahūrā, Nectar. شراب خانه sharāb-ḵẖāna, s. m. A tavern. شراب خوار sharāb-ḵẖẉār, Drinking wine, wine-drinker. شرابخواري sharāb-ḵẖẉār-ī, s. f. Wine-drinking, drunkenness. شراب زده sharāb-zada, Indisposed or affected with wine.
subs: s.m. A place where liquor is sold, a tavern; a distillery
s.m. Wine-drinking; — a wine-bibber, a drunkard
adj. Indisposed or affected with wine; intoxicated
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَراب (šarâb), from Arabic شَرَاب (šarāb).

ety: sharāb+an = S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. = sharābī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Drunken, intoxicated; — a wine-bibber, a drunkard; a drunken man.

ety: sharāb, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. & s.m. Drunken, intoxicated; — a wine-bibber, a drunkard; a drunken man.
more: adj. Intoxicated; drunk. sharābī , n. m. sharāban , n. f. A drunkard.
adj. Intoxicated. s. m. A drunkard.

ety: inf. n. of شرف 'to be or become exalted,' &c.
def: s.m. Highness of rank, &c., exaltation, eminence, excellence, rank, grandeur, glory, honour, dignity, nobility
more: n. m. شرف exalted. 1. Precedence ( tarjīh ). 2. Dignity; honor. 3. Excellence; eminence. sharf le jānā , v. n. To excel; outwit ( sabqat le jānā ).
s. m. Nobility, eminence, rank, excellency, honour.
subs: (-par), To bear away the palm (from), to excel, surpass

def: s. m. (dakh.) Money-changer, banker ( Harris ): see صراف ṣarrāf.

ety: for A. شرافة, inf. n. of شرف 'to be high, exalted, or eminent,' &c.
def: s.f. Being noble, nobility
more: n. f. See اشرافت Yeh bāt sharāfat se baīd hai . Prov. Saying. This is unworthy of a gentleman. sharāfat-panāh , lit. refuge of nobility. An epithet employed in addressing a subordinate officer.
subs: s.m. 'Asylum of nobility,' (in official correspondence) an epithet employed in addressing a subordinate officer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرَافَت (šarāfat), from Arabic شَرَافَة (šarāfa).

def: adj. Honourable, full of honour.

ety: inf. n. of سره 'to be vehemently desirous'
def: s.m. Vehement desire, greed, appetite, avidity, gluttony.
more: (Heb. To contend for power) s. m. Desire, appetite, avidity, gluttony.

def: n. f. Pl. of شرط q. v. Stipulations; requirements. sharāyat-i-iqrār-nāmah , n. f. The terms of a contract. sharāyat-i-tamhīdī , n. f. Preliminary conditions. sharāyat-i-sulah-nāmah , n. f. The terms of a treaty or of a compromise. sharāyat kā asar , G. G. The effect of (certain) provisions. sharāyat-i-mashrūtah , The stipulated conditions. sharāyat-i-nikāh , n. f. The terms of a marriage contract. sharāyat-i-wājib-ul-arz , Terms or stipulations of the village administration. ekaṭ kī sharāyat . G. G. The provisions of an act.
more: s. f. plur. (of شرط) 1. Agreements, stipulations. 2. Signs, marks.

def: s.f. pl. (of sharīt̤a = shart̤, q.v.), Agreements, stipulations, conditions, requirements; — signs, marks.

ety: S. शारिका; H. शारिक, सारिक, सारक
def: s.f. A starling; a mainā.
more: s. m. A species of nightingale, or a bird of a dark colour that talks like a parrot, perhaps the mainā or starling.
wety: Borrowed from English shark.

ety: acc. of sharʻ
def: adv. According to the law, legally
more: adv. According to the Mah. law; legally. sharan jāiz h. , v. n. To be good in (Mah.) law.
subs: adv. According to both the religious law of God and the law of society.

def: n. m. Making a chapping noise in eating ( suṛap ). shaṛappe lagānā, yā mārnā , v. a. To gulp down.
s.m. Sparks, or a spark, of fire (see sharāra).
more: (from شرر) s. m. A spark, or sparks of fire.

ety: contrac. of sharār, q.v.
def: s.m. Sparks of fire; malice
more: s. m. Sparks of fire. شرربار sharar-bār, Raining sparks of fire.

ety: for A. شرارة sharārat, n. of un. fr. sharār
def: s.m. A spark of fire; a flash, gleam.

ety: for A. شرارة, inf. n. of شرّ 'to be or become evil,' &c.
def: s.f. Wickedness, mischief, vice, depravity, villainy, atrocity
more: n. f. Mischief; wickedness. shararatan' ; A. H. sharārat se , adv. Wickedly; feloniously.
(from شرر) s. f. Wickedness, vice, mischief, depravity, villany.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرَارَت (šarārat), borrowed from Arabic شَرَارَة (šarāra), from شَرَّ (šarra).

ety: for A. شرارةً sharārataṉ, acc. of sharārat
def: adv. Wickedly, mischievously, &c.
n. f. A bird; Turdus ginginianus . (?).
s.f. pl. (of shiryān), The arteries.

ety: for A. شربة, n. of un. fr. sharb, inf. n. of شرب 'to drink'
def: s.m. A draught (of water, &c.), drink, beverage, cup; sherbet, sugar and water (the most com. signification); a dose of medicine, draught, potion
more: n. m. شرب drank. A sherbet; sugar and water. Āp to garm kar-ke sharbat pilāte haiṅ . Prov. You put me in a rage first and then give me cool sherbet (mollify me) sharbat pilānā , v. a. To give the barber a sherbet to drink and so seal the betrothal which he has arranged. Hin. sharbat-pilāī , n. f. The present made to the barber by the bride and bridegroom. sharbat ke se ghūṅṭ pīnā , v. a. 1. To gulp down as nectar. 2. To suffer meekly.
(from شرب) s. m. 1. Sherbet, beverage, drink. 2. The dose of a medicine. شربت دار sharbat-dār, s. m. A servant who has the charge of the water, wine, &c. a butler.
subs: (-ko), To give (a barber) sherbet to drink (and so seal the betrothal which he has arranged)
s.f. The present made by a bride and bridegroom to the barber who arranged the marriage
s.m. A servant who has charge of the water, wine, &c.; a butler
To gulp down as sherbet; (fig.) to suffer meekly.

ety: rel. n. fr. sharbat, q.v.
def: adj. Of or relating to sherbet; of the colour of sherbet (applied to things of an orange or pale yellow colour); juicy; — s.f. A kind of lime or lemon; orange or pale yellow colour; a kind of cloth or muslin; a kind of stone; a kind of seed.
more: n. f. 1. A kind of lime or lemon. 2. A kind of muslin. sharbatī , adj. 1. Of the color of sharbat . 2. Juicy ( rasīlā 1).
s. f. 1. A kind of lime, or lemon. 2. Name of a colour. ¶ adj. Of or relating to sherbet.

ety: act. part. of شرع 'to enter into water,' 'to make manifest,' &c.
def: s.m. An announcer of the law, legislator, lawgiver
more: n. m. A lawgiver. shāra-i-ām, shāre , n. m. A public road; highway.
(part. act. from شرع To pass a law, to enter the highway) 1. s. f. A highway. 2. s. m. A lawgiver. شارع عام shāri-iﻌ-āﻌmm, s. m. A publick road, highway.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شارع (šāri'), from Arabic شَارِع (šāriʕ).

def: adj. Ennobled; honored. sharf-yāb-i-mulāzemat, h. , v. n. To be honored by one's visit; to be admitted to audience.

ety: inf. n. of شرح 'to uncover, lay open,' &c.
def: s.f. An exposition, explanation, interpretation, a running commentary (on a work or text), annotation; description; — rate (of assessment, &c.), proportion, charge; allowance, pay
more: n. f. شرح laid open. 1. Explanation. 2. Commentary ( barnan, and ṭīkā , 11). 3. Rate of assessment; proportion; charge (H. dar , 2.). sharah-i-āb-pāshī , n. f. Irrigation rate. sharah-bandī , n. f. A table of rates. sharah pānī , n. f. Water rate. sharah-i-paunṭoṭe , n. f. Rate of town duty. sharah chaṛhānā , v. a. To annotate; write a commentary. sharah khūrāk-i-gavāhāṅ , Rate of allowances to witnesses. sharah-i-sūd , n. f. Rate of interest. sharah k. , v. n. 1. To define; determine with precision. 2. To detail ( tafsīl k .). 3. To assess; fix the rate. sharah lagān , Rent rate. sharah māmūlī , n. f. The customary or usual rate. sharah-i-muqarrarah , n. f. A fixed rate. sharah nahr , n. f. Canal rates. sharah-vār , adv. As detailed; explicitly; at length; in full. ujrat kī sharah , n. f. Rate of fees, or wages. ba-sharah-i-zail , adv. On the terms herein after set forth; as hereinafter…
s. f. 1. Explanation, commentary, annotation, description. 2. Allowance, pay, rate. شرحوار sharḥ-wār, adv. Explicitly. شرح کشاف sharḥ-i-kashshāf, A lucid explanation.
subs: (-par), To write a commentary (on), to annotate
(-kī), To explain, define, determine; to detail; to fix or determine the rate (of), to assess
adv. Explicitly, distinctly, clearly, plainly; in detail, at length; as detailed
adv. As hereinafter shown, as detailed or shown below, in form and manner following

def: adj. Of or relating to the religion or law; accordant to the religion or law, legal, lawful, legitimate
more: adj. Conformable or according to the shara ; lawful; constitutional. sharaī paijāmā , n. m. Drawers reaching down to the ankle, worn by the strictly pious. sharaī dāṛhī , A short beard, as prescribed in the sharā . sharٴaī qasam , Mah. A solemn oath. sharaī kuṭṭan , Slang. n. m. A lawful pimp; the priest who ties the nuptial knot.
adj. 1. Conformable to the sharﻌ, lawful, religious. 2. ( met. ) s. f. Tight drawers.
s. (dakh.) Long drawers: see شلوار shalwār (Qanoon-e-islam).
subs: s.m., and sharʻī, s.f. Tight drawers reaching down to the ankle (worn by the strictly pious and by women)
s.f. A short beard (as prescribed in the sharʻ).

ety: for A. شريعة, v.n. fr. شرع 'to make apparent,' &c.
def: s.f. The religious law of God (cf. sharʻ); law, ordinance, statute; justice, equity.
more: (from شرع) s. f. 1. The laws of Muḥammad. 2. Law, justice, equity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرِیعَت (šarī'at), from Arabic شَرِيعَة (šarīʕa). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi شَرِیعَت (śry't).

ety: v.n. fr. شرف 'to be high, or eminent,' &c.
def: adj. Of high rank or dignity, exalted, eminent, honourable, noble, of good family, high-born; possessing glory or dignity; legitimate; — a title of honour of any descendant of the Prophet, as also of the rulers of Mecca; a prince, a chief or head (e.g. sharīf-ě-qaum, 'chief or head of a tribe or caste')
more: adj. شرف exalted. 1. Of high rank or dignity; noble ( baṛā 5-7). 2. High-born; well-born ( khāndānī , 3). 3. Legitimate ( halālī ). 4. Sacred ( pāk , 2). Makka sharīf. sharīf , n. m. 1. A title given to the rulers of Mecca; a prince. 2. Cor. of Eng. sheriff. sharīf khāndān kā, khāndān kā sharīf, qaum kā sharīf , adj. Of high birth; of a good family or stock. sharīf-i-qaum , n. m. The head of a caste. sharīf o razīl . High and low. bāshindgān-i-sharīf , n. m. Respectable inhabitants. zāt-i-sharīf , ( Ironic .) His Satanic majesty; a thorough knave.
(from شرف) adj. Noble, eminent, honourable. شريف النفس sharīfu-n-nafs, adj. Noble of soul. شريف مکه sharīf-i-makka, The prince of Mecca.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرِیف (šarīf), from Arabic شَرِيف (šarīf).

ety: fr. sharīf
def: s.m. The custard-apple, Annona squamosa.
more: n. m. Custard apple ( Anona squamosa ).
s. m. A custard apple. (Annona squamosa).

ety: act. part. of شرح 'to disclose,' &c.
def: s.m. Expositor, explainer, commentator, annotator, interpreter.
more: n. m. شرح explaining. A commentator; annotator.
(part. act. from شرح) s. Commentator, annotator, expositor.

ety: act. part. of شرق 'to rise' (as the sun
def: s.m. The sun.
more: (part. act. of شرق) s. m. The sun.

ety: v.n. fr. شرك 'to share,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Confederate, united, joint, concerned (in); — a sharer, participator, partaker (with); a partner, co-partner, an associate, a colleague, comrade, ally, a confederate, an accomplice, accessory; a member (of a community); a friend
more: adj. Joint; united shāmil , 2, 4). sharīk , n. m. 1. A member of a community. 2. A co-sharer; co-partner ( hissedār ). 3. An accessory. 4. A confederate ( rafīq ). sharīk-ul-rāë . Of the same opinion. sharīk-i-ranj o rāhat , A partner in sunshine and rain, for weal or woe. sharīk ba-vaqt-i-jurm . G. G. An accomplice in a crime. sharīk-i-jurm , G. G. Particeps criminis. sharīk-i-jurm k . To implicate or include in a crime. sharīk-i-hāl See shāmil-i-hāl. sharīk rahnā, shāmil rahnā , v. n. To live together. sharīk-i-shikmī , n. m. Coparcener; joint sublessee; a joint owner. sharīk k. , v. a. 1. To include ( shāmil k . 3). 2. To make one a partner in. sharīk-i-majma . A member of an assembly. sharīk h. , v. n. See shāmil h. , 1. To join in; cooperate with ( shāmil h. , 4). 2. To have a share in; to be a partner ( sājhī h .); to have or possess in common; go halves; gosnacks. jurm meṅ sharīk …
(from شرک) s. m. 1. A partner, associate, accomplice, comrade, ally. شريک هونا sharīk honā, To partake, participate. 2. A friend.
subs: adj. & s.m. Of the same opinion; — a person of the same opinion (as another)
s.m. Joint owner; coparcener; joint sub-lessee
v.t. To make (one) a sharer or partner (in, -meṅ), to include (in)
v.n. To join (in, -meṅ), to co-operate (with), to partake (of), to participate (in); to be a partner or associate (of, -kā); to have or possess in common; to be an accessory (to, -meṅ), to abet

ety: v.n. fr. شرّ 'to be evil,' &c.
def: adj. Evil, bad, corrupt, wicked, vicious, depraved.
more: adj. Z. salia, salyā bad. Wicked ( burā , 1).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرِیر (šarīr), from Arabic شَرِير (šarīr).

def: s.f. prob. a mistake for sharīʻat, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of شرق 'to rise' (as the sun
def: s.m. Rising (of the sun); the place of rising, the east (syn. mashriq)
more: n. m. 1. Rising (of the sun). 2. East ( pūrab ). sharq se garb tak . From East to West.

def: adj. Eastern, oriental.
more: adj. Eastern. sharqī zabān , G. G. An oriental language. ulūm-i-sharqī , G. G. Oriental learning.

def: s.m. (corr. of sharq-istān) The place of rising (of the sun), the east; — (corr. of shirk-istān) 'The country of unbelievers,' India, Asia.

ety: Pehl. sharm; Zend fharema
def: s.f. Shame, bashfulness, modesty
more: n. f. Shame (حيا). 1. Āṅkh meṅ thī sharm, dil kī thī narm . The eye with shame suffused, the heart with pity fused. 2. Syām sakhin par, sakhī Syām par abīr uḍāë muskāve, Aiso phāg macho yā Brij meṅ, hayā saram uṭh gaī, gaī re! Syam on the maids, the maids on Syam with wanton laugh red powder throw! Such carnival as Brij hath seen! gone is her maiden shame! woe woe!
s. f. Bashfulness, modesty, shame. شرم ناک sharm-nāk, adj. Bashful, modest.
subs: (-ko), To feel shame; to be abashed
(-ko), To put to shame, to make (one) ashamed
(-kī), To preserve from shame or disgrace, to guard the honour (of the beard, &c.)
and sharm-sār, adj. Stricken with shame; bashful; ashamed, abashed
and sharm khānā (-kī), To feel ashamed, to be bashful or shy; to be abashed
and jāʼe sharm hai, It is a shame
vulg. sharm-gīṅ, adj. Bashful, ashamed, modest, abashed
(-ko) = sharm ānā, and sharm khānā, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Shame-faced, bashful, shy; — a shame-faced or bashful person
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرْم (šarm), from Middle Persian 𐭱𐭥𐭬 (šʿm /⁠šarm⁠/).

ety: sharm, q.v.+Prk. आलू = S. आलु+कः
def: adj. Shame-faced, bashful, &c. ( = sharmanda and sharmīlā).

ety: sharm, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. To feel shame, be bashful, to be ashamed, be abashed, to blush; — v.t. To make (one) ashamed, to put to shame.
more: v. n. 1. To be abashed, ashamed; to feel ashamed. 1. Kuāriyoṅ kī tarah sharmātā hai, kab yeh mard hai? Ran liyoṅ meṅ yeh nigoṛā hījṛā ātā nahīṅ . Wom. Rāhat. As shame-faced as a girl, a man he cannot be, 'Mong women you this wretched eunuch never see. 2. Khāne meṅ sharm nahīṅ karte . Prov. Saying. It's no shame to eat and drink.
v. a. To put one to shame; put to the blush. Khudā tum ko sharmāë! God give you some sense of shame!
v. n. To be abashed, to feel shame, to be ashamed.

ety: imperf. part. of sharmīdan; — sharm, q.v.+anda = Zend aff. añṭ = S. अन्त्
def: part. adj. Ashamed, abashed, shame-faced, bashful, modest, blushing
more: adj. Blushing; bashful; abashed; ashamed. sharmindah h. , v. n. To be ashamed, etc; to blush.
(part. pres. of شرميدن) adj. Blushing, abashed, ashamed, bashful, modest.
subs: v.t. To make (one) ashamed, to put to shame, put to the blush
(-se), To feel ashamed of oneself (on account of, or with respect to, a matter); to be ashamed, be bashful, to blush.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Shame-facedness, bashfulness, modesty; shame, disgrace; repentance
more: n. f. Shame-facedness. sharmindgī uṭhānā , v. n. To endure shame or disgrace.
s. f. 1. Bashfulness, modesty. 2. Shame, repentance, disgrace.

def: adv. Through shame. sharmā-sharmī iqrār kar līyā hai, jī to nahīṅ chāhtā thā .
adj. = sharmālū and sharmanda, qq.v. adj. Shame-faced, bashful, &c. ( = sharmanda and sharmīlā).

ety: perf. part. of sharmānā
def: part. adj. (f. sharmāʼī), Ashamed, abashed, smitten with shame.
The privities.
adj. Bashful, ashamed, modest, coy.
s. f. Bashfulness, shame.

ety: sharm, q.v.+Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = sharmālū, and sharmanda, qq.v. adj. Shame-faced, bashful, &c. ( = sharmanda and sharmīlā).
adj. Abashed; ashamed.
s.f. = shār, and shārak, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of شرّ 'to become evil or bad,' &c.
def: s.f. Evil, wrongdoing, injustice, badness, corruptness, wickedness, viciousness, depravity, malignancy
more: s. f. Wickedness, depravity, malignancy, evil.
subs: To raise a commotion or disturbance, cause a breach of the peace, to make a riot
s.m. Wickedness and mischief; a breach of the peace, a riot.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شر (šarr), from Arabic شَرّ (šarr).

ety: rel. n. fr. sharr, q.v.
def: adj. Vicious; quarrelsome (cf. sharīr).

ety: inf. n. of شرط 'to impose a thing as a condition,' &c.
def: s.f. A condition, stipulation, agreement, term, provision, engagement, bargain; a wager, bet; (in Gram.) a conditional conjunction; — sharat̤, or shart̤, s.f. Sign, token, mark, signal
more: n. f. 1. Condition; stipulation; engagement; term; provision; proviso. Hai shart ki abhī nālīsh kar dūṅ . 2. A bet; wager. 3. ( Gram .) A conditional conjunction. shart bāṅdhnā, badnā, budnā, k., yā lagānā , v. a. 1. To make terms; stipulate; provide; bargain. 2. To bind by contract; contract an obligation. 3. To bet ( bāzī badnā ). 1. Murdoṅ se shart bāṅdh-kar sotā hai . Wom. Prov. Saying. He sleeps as if he had made a bet with the dead (as to who could sleep longest). 2. Shekh jī jāte haiṅ rindoṅ ko nasīhat karne, Shart budtā hūṅ ki pagṛī jo salāmat rah jāe . Asīr. The holy Shaikh's gone 'mongst the rioters to preach, With turban whole, I bet you, he his home dont reach. shart-i-tamhīdī , n. f. A preliminary condition. shart-i-khās , n. f. A specific condition. shart-i-zurūrī , n. f. An indispensable condition; a sine qua non . shart o jazā , ( Gram .) The condition and the consequence…

(Heb. and Chald. An incision) s. f. 1. Condition, agreement, stipulation, bargain. 2. Wager. 3. Sign, mark, signal. شرط برت shart̤-bart, s. f. Advantage, success, profit, succeeding in oneʼs undertaking. کچهہ شرط برت هوٴي kuchh shart̤-bart hūʼī, Did you get any thing by, &c. شرط کرنا shart̤-karnā, To stipulate, to agree, to bargain.
subs: To enter into mutual obligations; to wager

or shart̤ badnā or budnā (-ke sāth, 'with' a person; -kī, 'for' a thing), To make terms (with), to stipulate, provide (for), bargain (for); to bind by contract; to contract an obligation; to lay a wager (with), to bet
s.f. Succeeding in an undertaking, success; advantage, benefit, gain
(-ke sāth), To stipulate (with), &c. ( = shart̤ bāṅdhnā, q.v.)
s.f. The condition and its complement or correlative, the condition and the consequence
conj. On condition that, provided that.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَرْط (šart), borrowed from Arabic شَرْط (šarṭ). Compare Punjabi ਸ਼ਰਤ (śarat) / شَرْط (śart̤).

def: To stipulate; bargain, bet, wager.

ety: rel. n. fr. shart̤
def: adj. Conditional, contingent, provisional; — shart̤ī, adv. Certainly, undoubtedly; — s.f. A lottery.
more: adj. Contingent; conditional; provisionary; provisional. shartī , adv. Certainly; undoubtedly. Ek din terā sīs kaṭāūṅ, roz laṛāī kartī, Jab tak jīūṅ is duniyā meṅ saṅg na chhoṛūṅ shartī . Play. istehqāq-i-shartīyah . A contingent interest. jumla-i-sharti'yah , ( Gram .) A conditional clause.
1. adj. Conditional. 2. s. f. A lottery.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شُرْطِيّ (šurṭiyy).

ety: for A. شرطية, f. of sharti
def: adj. Conditional, contingent, provisional; — shart̤ī, adv. Certainly, undoubtedly; — s.f. A lottery.
s.m. = shalwār, q.v. s.m. Trousers, drawers (reaching to the feet), breeches.

ety: prob. for A. شرزة sharzat, fr. شرز 'to be violent,' &c.
def: adj. Powerful, strong, vehement, violent; fierce, enraged; raging, roaring, ravenous (a lion or tiger, &c.); — s.m. A ravenous beast of prey, a raging lion or tiger, &c.
more: adj. Raging, fierce, roaring, powerful (a lion or tiger). s. Name of a ravenous beast more powerful than the lion.

def: s.m. Urine
more: shāsha, s. Urine. شاش دان shāsh-dān, Bladder, urinal.

ety: Pehl. shash; Zend khshvas; S. षष्
def: adj. Six
more: adj. Six. shash-pahlū , adj. Having six sides; hexagonal.
subs: or shash-panlū, and shash-jihat, adj. & s.m. Six-sided, hexagonal; — a hexagon; a cube
adj. Having six compartments; six-roomed; having six grooves (a rifle); six-barrelled
s.m. 'Six doors'; a cube, a die; — a point of the table at the game of nard from which one cannot extricate himself
v.n. To stand amazed, be astounded, &c.
adj. Six-monthly, half-yearly; — s.f. The space of six months; the half-yearly accounts of a village
s.m. lit. 'Sixes and fives,' anxious thought, deliberation, anxiety, uneasiness; uncertainty, hesitation; confusion, bewilderment, perplexity

def: s.m. Urine
n. m. 1. A cube; a die. 2. A point of the table at the game of nard from which one cannot extricate himself. shash-dar , adj. Confounded (حيران 2).
more: (شش Six) s. m. 1. A cube, a die. 2. adj. Astonished, confounded, ashamed.

def: adj. Half-yearly. shash-māhī , n. f. See chha-māhī , under chhai. shash-māhī imtihān . Half-yearly examination. shash o panj , n. m. lit . six or five. 1. See udheṛ-bun . 2. Uncertainty; hesitation ( dubdhā , 1). shash o panj meṅ , adv. At sixes and sevens.
adj. Sixtieth (used in comp. after another numeral).
adj. (f. -ā or -ī), Sixth.
s.f. The sixth day of a lunar fortnight; — an epithet of Durgā.
s.f. The sixth day of a lunar fortnight; — an epithet of Durgā.
s.f. The sixth day of a lunar fortnight; — an epithet of Durgā.
adj. Sixty.

ety: shash, q.v.+Zend aff. ma = S. ma
def: adj. Sixth.

ety: for شست shast; Zend khshvaśti; S. षष्टि
def: adj. Sixty.

ety: rt. Zend had = S. शद्; cf. nishast
def: s.f. Aim; the sight (of a gun, &c.); the sighted ruler of a plane-table; a thumb-stall (used by archers); the handle of a bow; a large fishing-hook (not used in angling with the hand, but left attached to a strong line which is fastened to the shore)
more: n. f. 1. Aim ( sīdh , 2). 2. The 'Sight' used in a plane table survey. 3. The handle of a bow. 4. A thumbstall. 5. A large fishing rod left attached to a strong line which is fastened to the shore. shast bāṅdhnā, yā lagānā , v. a. To aim at; to present a gun, etc. shist-paṭrī . Plane table survey.

s. f. 1. Aim. 2. A large fishing hook (not used in angling with the hand, but left attached to a strong line which is fastened to the shore). 3. The handle of a bow. 4. A thumbstal (used to defend the thumb in pulling the string of a bow). شست باندهنا shast bāndhnā, To aim. شست جسته shast-justa, Shot from the bow (an arrow).
subs: s.f. Assessment on land; the balance of dues uncollected
(or shist) bāṅdhnā or lagānā (-par), To take aim (at), to present (a gun), to take a sight (with the ruler of a plane-table)
part. Shot from the bow (an arrow).

def: s. m. Argument, debate, dispute, discussion, discourse.
s. f. Place or magazine of arms.
(dakh. corrupt. of شـﮩد shahd ) s. Honey ( Harris ).

ety: used for shash before hard letters; and euphon. changed to shaḍ before soft letters
def: adj. Six
subs: s.m. The six parts of the body (viz. the arms, the legs, the head, and the waist); — the six auxiliary or supplementary branches (aṅg) of sacred science (i.e. Grammar; Prosody; Pronunciation; the meanings of unusual terms; Astronomy; and the ritual of the Hindū religion); — a set of six particular articles of offering at the first oblation of rice, &c. to the manes of deceased ancestors
and vulg. shaṭ-ānan, adj. & s.m. Six-faced; — an epithet of Kārttikeya, the son of Śiva and god of war
adj. & s.m. Six-footed; — a bee; a louse
s.m. The six seasons (see r̤itu); — shaḍ-ras, vulg. shaṭ-ras, s.m. The six flavours or tastes (see ras)
vulg. shaṭ-darśan, and shaṇ-darśan, s.m. The six systems of philosophy ( = shaṭ-śāstr, q.v.); — a man who is versed in the six systems of philosophy
s.m. The six philosophical systems of the Hindūs (viz. the Nyāya; the Vaiśeshika; the Mīmāṅsā; the Vedānta; the Sāṅkhya; and the Pāṭanjala)

s.m. pl. The six acts or duties enjoined on a Brāhmaṇ (viz. teaching the Vedas, holy study, offering sacrifices, conducting them for others, giving and accepting gifts); — the six acts allowable to a Brāhmaṇ for subsistence (viz. gleaning, accepting gifts, asking alms, agriculture, trade, tending cattle, or, according to some, lending money at interest); — six acts that may be performed by means of magical texts as taught in the Tantras (viz. killing, infatuating, enthralling, expelling, exciting animosity, and the stopping or privation of any faculty); — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the above six acts; an adept in the Tantra magical rites
or shaṭ-konā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Hexangular; — a hexagon, any six-angled figure; the thunderbolt of Indra
adj. & s.m. Six-fold, six times; — an assemblage of six qualities or properties
vulg. shaṭ-vadan, adj. & s.m. = shaḍ-ānan, q.v.
adj. Of six kinds or descriptions; six-fold, six times; in six ways

def: s.f. (dialec.) = ćhaṭāṅk, q.v. s.f. The sixteenth part of a ser, two ounces (avoirdupois, nearly).

ety: act. part. of شطر 'to withdraw from one's family,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Withdrawing far away from one's family; vexing or injuring relatives or friends; sharp, clever, astute, cunning, sly, mischievous, roguish; vicious, wanton; — one who thwarts or harasses his relations; — a sharper, a clever or cunning person, &c.; — a chess-player; (in Persian) a messenger, runner, courier.
more: adj. Smart. See H. chālāk. Yār-i-shātir hūṅ, na bār-i-khātir . Prov. A friend to keep you glad, and not to make you sad. shātir , n. m. A chess-player.
(part. act. of شطر) adj. 1. Wanton. 2. Clever, sly. 3. (substantively) A chess-player. 4. A messenger.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَاطِر (šātir), from Arabic شَاطِر (šāṭir).

ety: inf. n. of شتم 'to revile,' &c.
def: s.m. Reviling, vilifying, upbraiding; reproach, contumely, contempt; outrage, injury, villainy.
more: s. m. Contumely, reproach, contempt, outrage, injury, villany.

def: n. m. lit . six-footed. A bee; louse. shaṭ-kon ; H. chhaë-konyā , adj. Hexagonal.

ety: the P. shatraṅg arabicised; S. चतुरंग
def: s.f. Chess
more: n. f. P. shatraṅg . Chess. Shat-ranj nahīṅ, sad ranj hai . Prov. It's not a game of chess, but cares a hundred bought. shatraṅj-bāz , n. m. A chess-player. shatraṅj-bāzī , n. f. Playing at chess.
(Pers. شترنگ Sans. चतुरंग ) s. f. Chess. شطرنج باز shat̤ranj-bāz, A chess-player.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَطْرَنْج (šatranj), borrowed from Arabic شَطْرَنْج (šaṭranj), borrowed from Classical Persian چَتْرَنگ (čatrang), from Middle Persian 𐭰𐭠𐭲𐭫𐭠𐭭𐭢 (cʾtlʾng /⁠čatrang⁠/, “chess”), borrowed from Sanskrit चतुरङ्ग (caturaṅga, “comprising elephants, chariots, cavalry, and infantry”), from चतुर् (catúr, “four”) + अङ्ग (áṅga, “limb, member”), literally “four limbs; four members”.

ety: fr. shat̤ranj
def: s.f. A kind of chequered carpet, a carpet made of cotton, a darī (q.v.)
more: n. f. A carpet made of cotton.
s. f. A kind of carpet. شطرنجي باف shat̤ranjī-bāf, s. m. A carpet-weaver.

def: s. Bank of a river.

ety: for A. شتي shattā, q.v.
def: adj. Distinct, different, various.
more: (from شتّ Was separate or dispersed) adj. Distinct, different, various.
adj. (dakh.) 1. See حراف ḥarrāf. 2. See مکاره makkāra. 3. See قحبه ḳaḥba.

ety: v.n. fr. شتّ 'to become dissolved,' &c.
def: adj. Distinct, different, various.
adj. Meretricious, artful, shameless, lascivious, lewd, obscene.
more: Mah. Wom. adj. Artful ( chālāk ). Baṛī shattāh haiṅ bī, ḍarye un jurvoṅ ke dīde se! Chalī haiṅ ghūrne mardoṅ ko hīle se ziārat ke . Wom. Rāhat. An artful woman, dear! such eyes! my friends, beware! To worship her pretext, to ogle men her care.
adj. (superl. of شطح) Meretricious, lascivious, obscene. See شتاه.

def: Vulgar, n. m. A blow ( chāṅṭā ).
adj. prob. corr. of شطاه shat̤t̤āh, q.v. adj. Six
more: adj. perhaps for شطاح shaṭṭāḥ, q. v.
subs: s.m. The six parts of the body (viz. the arms, the legs, the head, and the waist); — the six auxiliary or supplementary branches (aṅg) of sacred science (i.e. Grammar; Prosody; Pronunciation; the meanings of unusual terms; Astronomy; and the ritual of the Hindū religion); — a set of six particular articles of offering at the first oblation of rice, &c. to the manes of deceased ancestors
and vulg. shaṭ-ānan, adj. & s.m. Six-faced; — an epithet of Kārttikeya, the son of Śiva and god of war
adj. & s.m. Six-footed; — a bee; a louse
s.m. The six seasons (see r̤itu); — shaḍ-ras, vulg. shaṭ-ras, s.m. The six flavours or tastes (see ras)
vulg. shaṭ-darśan, and shaṇ-darśan, s.m. The six systems of philosophy ( = shaṭ-śāstr, q.v.); — a man who is versed in the six systems of philosophy
s.m. The six philosophical systems of the Hindūs (viz. the Nyāya; the Vaiśeshika; the Mīmāṅsā; the Vedānta; the Sāṅkhya; and the Pāṭanjala)

s.m. pl. The six acts or duties enjoined on a Brāhmaṇ (viz. teaching the Vedas, holy study, offering sacrifices, conducting them for others, giving and accepting gifts); — the six acts allowable to a Brāhmaṇ for subsistence (viz. gleaning, accepting gifts, asking alms, agriculture, trade, tending cattle, or, according to some, lending money at interest); — six acts that may be performed by means of magical texts as taught in the Tantras (viz. killing, infatuating, enthralling, expelling, exciting animosity, and the stopping or privation of any faculty); — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the above six acts; an adept in the Tantra magical rites
or shaṭ-konā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Hexangular; — a hexagon, any six-angled figure; the thunderbolt of Indra
adj. & s.m. Six-fold, six times; — an assemblage of six qualities or properties
vulg. shaṭ-vadan, adj. & s.m. = shaḍ-ānan, q.v.
adj. Of six kinds or descriptions; six-fold, six times; in six ways

def: s.m. = کهٿوانگ khaṭwāṅg, q.v.

ety: contrac. of shauhar, q.v.
def: s.m. A husband.
wety: Borrowed from English show.

def: n. m. Husband. shauhar kī zindgī tak . During coverture.

ety: Pehl. shǒʼi; Zend khshudra, 'seed'; S. क्षुद्र
def: s.m. A husband.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شوهر (šawhar).

def: adj. Of or belonging to a husband, husband's.
adj. Of, or belonging to the husband.

ety: inf. n. of شوق 'to excite desire,' &c.
def: s.m. Desire, yearning, deep longing, predilection (for, -kā), inclination, affection, love; fancy (for), pleasure (in); taste; ardour, zeal, eagerness, avidity; alacrity, gaiety, cheerfulness; curiosity
more: n. m. 1. Interest; ardour; pleasure; taste; fancy (اشتياق). 1. Shauq dād-Ilāhī hai . Prov. Saying. Taste is a gift from Heaven. 2. Imārat kā baṛā shauq hai . He has a great fancy for architecture. 2. The part of an epistle which follows the complimentary address. shauq se , adv. With pleasure; with all one's heart. shauqīyah , adj. Loving; endearing; affectionate. shauqīyah-khat ; H. prem-pātī . A love letter.

(Heb. q. d. motion or tendency towards any point, from To walk hastily, to run about) s. m. 1. Desire, inclination, love, eagerness, avidity, fondness. 2. Gayety, cheerfulness. 3. Curiosity. شوق ذوق shauḳ-ẕauḳ, s. m. Pleasure, delight, gratification.
subs: adv. With alacrity, &c., with pleasure, gladly, cheerfully; at pleasure, according to (one's) wish or desire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شوق (šawq), from Arabic شَوْق (šawq).

def: s.m. Hellebore; hemlock.

ety: for A. شوکة, n. of un. fr. shauk, inf. n. of شوك 'to pierce,' &c.
def: s.f. A thorn; — power, might, majesty, grandeur, magnificence, dignity, state, pomp
more: (from شوک) s. f. State, dignity, magnificence, majesty. شوکت البعير shaukatu-l-baʼīﻌr, A thistle.

ety: rel. n. fr. shauq
def: adj. & s.m. Desirous, loving, amorous; cheerful; — a desirer, &c. (see shauqīn).

ety: shauq, q.v.+īn = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. & s.m. Desirous, loving, amorous; lascivious; fond (of), intent (upon), devoted (to), eager; a lover (of), fancier (of); an amateur; an enthusiast.
more: adj. Fond of; devoted to. shauqīn , n. m. 1. A fancier; amateur; enthusiast. 2. See البيلا n. 2, 4. charrāyā-huā shauq , n. m. Intense desire. Charrāyā huā shauq bhalā kab māntā hai, chanḍū- khāne meṅ le hī pahuṅchā . Oudh Punch.
adj. 1. Intent upon, desirous, eager. 2. Lascivious.

ety: fr. shauqī, after the model of the A. fem.
def: adj. Amorous, loving, tender, affectionate, &c.; — s.m. The part of an epistle which follows the complimentary address.
more: s. m. The part of an epistle which follows the complimental address.

def: s. m. plur. (of شايبة) Impure things, impurities.
s.f. pl. (of shārěʻ), High roads.

ety: v.n. fr. شول 'to rise,' &c.
def: s.m. The tenth month of the Mohammadan year (the month next after Ramazān; — on the first day of Shawwāl the festival of Bairām or ʻId is celebrated, and it is customary to make presents, &c.).
more: n. m. The 10th month of the Mah. year, the first of which, following Ramzān , is held as a festival at which presents are made.

s. m. The tenth month of the Muhammadan year; on the first day of which the festival of Bairām, or īﻌd is celebrated, being the first day after the musalmān Lent; and, on this day it is customary to make presents, &c.

ety: Pārsī shāyaṭ; 3rd pers. sing. aor. of shāyistan, rt. shāy; see shāyista
def: adv. It may be, perhaps, perchance, possibly, probably
more: adv. شايستن to become. Perhaps; mayhap; probably; possibly; in posse. shāyad ki bāshad . Perhaps; it may be so.
(3d pers. sing. aor. of شايستن) adv. May be, possibly, probably, perchance, perhaps. شايد که باشد shāyad ki bāshad, Perhaps it may be.
subs: It may be that it is, it may be so, perhaps it is.

def: adj. شرق desire. Zealous ardent shāëqīn' , pl.; shauqīn , sing. Lovers of; votaries
more: (part. act. of شوق Heb. To desire) adj. Full of desire, desirous, lascivious, fond.

def: adj. Worthy; desirable. (اُچت 1-3). Āp ko yeh bāt shāyāṅ nahīṅ. shāyāṅ h. , v. n. To become; suit; fit.

ety: imperf. part. of shāyistan; see shāyista
def: adj. Proper, fit, suitable; worthy; desirable; agreeable; lawful, legal, allowed, permitted; possible; contingent
subs: (-ko), To be proper (for), be suitable or fitting (to), to suit, become, befit.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شایان (šāyān).

def: s.m. pl. (of shait̤ān), Devils, demons.

ety: perf. part. of shāyistan, fr. shāy = shāh˚ = Zend khshay˚, rt. khshi = S. क्षि+stan = hastan
def: adj. Worthy, honourable; fitting, proper, suitable; decent; useful; expedient; lawful, unobjectionable; affable, well-bred, courteous, polite; gentle, docile, meek, quiet
more: adj. 1. Proper ( ṭhīk , 4, 6, 7). 2. Courteous; well-bred; polite. 3. Quiet; meek; docile.
adj. Worthy, honourable, suitable, proper, expedient, wellbred, polite. شايسته مزاج shāyasta-mizāj, adj. Affable, mild, goodtempered.

ety: abst. s. fr. shāyista, q.v.
def: s.f. Suitableness, propriety, fitness, aptitude; consistency; conveniency, expediency; affability, urbanity, politeness, courtesy
more: n. f. 1. Fitness; suitableness; aptitude. 2. Affability; urbanity; politeness; good manners. shāistgī se , adv. Duly; fitly; properly.
s. f. Affability, fitness, aptitude, propriety, expediency, consistency.

def: adj. Divulged; issued; published. shāya h. , v. n. To be spread abroad, pro pagated.
more: part. act. (of شيع Heb. To exclaim) 1. Divulged, published, diffused, notorious. 2. Distressed.

ety: act. part of شذّ 'to be or become apart,' &c.
def: adj. Uncommon, unusual, rare, extraordinary, exceptional, abnormal, irregular, anomalous; wonderful, miraculous; — adv. Rarely, seldom
more: adj. شذ became separate. 1. Uncommon; rare. 2. ( Gram .) Irregular; anomalous; exceptional.
(part. act. from شذّ To remain alone or separate from others) adj. Uncommon, miraculous, wonderful; irregular or anomalous (in grammar), an exception. adv. Seldom.

def: (from شعب) s. m. The eighth Arabian month. (The 14th day of it is the festival called برات barāt, q. v.: on this day, an angel is supposed to examine a record, on which is written the names of the living).

ety: v.n. fr. شعر 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. Mark, signal, sign, countersign, password, parole; habit, custom, practice; method, manner; a garment worn next to the body, an under-garment (opp. to dis̤ār); — (shěʻār is much used in comp. to form adj., e.g. jādū-shěʻār, 'magical'; — karam-shěʻār, 'adorned with, or distinguished for, generosity').
more: (from شعر Heb. ) s. m. 1. An under garment. 2. Mark, signal, sign. 3. Habit, custom, method, manner: (much used in compos. as) جادوشعار jādū-shiʼāﻌr, Magical.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شعار (ši'ār), from Arabic شِعَار (šiʕār).

ety: fr. shěʻār
def: adj. Of or relating to custom, &c.; customary, habitual.
more: ( In comp .) adj. Relating to custom, etc.
adj. Of or relating to custom, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شعاری (ši'ārī), derived from Arabic شِعَار (šiʕār).

def: (v.n. fr. شعب 'to collect, or draw together,' &c.), s.m.(?) A road in a mountain, a pass, defile; a ravine; a depressed tract, a hollow in the ground.

ety: said to be a contrac. of nasheb; but cf. شعب sheʻb
def: s.m. A descent, declivity; a hollow; the lower part or bottom (of a mountain, &c.); — adv. At the bottom (of), under, below.
more: s. m. 1. A descent, declivity. 2. adv. Under, below.

ety: Pehl. shet; Zend khshaêta, fr. khshiṭ, rt. khshi; S. क्षित्, rt. क्षि (क्षयति)
def: adj. & s.m. Bright, shining, clear; — light; the sun; fascination (cf. ḵẖẉurshed).

ety: perf. part. of sheftan, another form of shuftan; rt. Zend khshub = S. क्षुभ्
def: part. & s.m. Mad; distracted (with love), infatuated, enamoured (of, -kā); — a distracted or infatuated person, a lover
more: part. pass. (of شيفتن) Distracted, infatuated, enamoured, mad.
subs: s.m. The smooth red peach; an apricot (cf. shaft-rang)
(-kā), To be mad (upon, or after), to be enamoured (of), be desperately in love (with).

ety: pl. of sheḵẖ
def: s.m. (dialec.), Shaikhs, &c.
subs: s.m. A town inhabited by Shaikhs or descendants of the prophet.

def: s. m. A town inhabited by descendants of the prophet.

ety: fr. shaiḵẖ
def: s.f. Boasting, bragging; boast
more: n. f. Vaunting; boast ( ḍīṅg ). 1. Shekhī kā muṅh kālā . Prov. Pride has a black face. (Pride goeth before a fall). 2. Shekhī ke māre mare jāte haiṅ . Prov. Saying. He is eaten up with pride. shekhī-bāz, shekhī-khorā , n. m. A boaster; braggart. Shekhī-khore se kahā, 'terā ghar jaltā hai.' Us ne kahā 'balā se, merī shekhī to mere pās hai .' Prov. To a braggart I once said, 'Your house is all in flames.' Says he, 'No matter, Sir, my brag at least's not gone.' shekhī baghārnā, k., yā mārnā, shekhī meṅ ānā, yā shekhī jatānā , v. a. To boast; brag. Do agvāṛe, do pichhvāṛe, chār khaṛe chaubāre, Chār chār kī chār chaukṛī, jiske pīchhe gyāre, Das ab āveṅ, das phir jāveṅ, chaudhe chār aṭhāre, Itne to tujhe phireṅ būjhte, jispe shekhī māre! Sautan kī larāī . Quarrels of co-wives. Play. Two front, two rear, and four in upper story wait, Four sets four each, and eleven besides with you to mate, Ten…
subs: and sheḵẖī jatāna = sheḵẖī mārnā, q.v.
(-kī), The boasting to be taken out (of); to have (one's) pride humbled.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَیْخِی (šayxī).

ety: shaiḵẖ+ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Son of a shekh (used in contempt).

ety: inf. n. of شعر 'to know'; 'to speak in verse'
def: s.m. Poetry, verse; a verse, a couplet
more: n. m. 1. Poetry. 2. A verse; couplet. sher-khuānī , n. f. Reading poetry. sher kahnā yā banānā , v. a. To compose verses. sher-goī ; H. chhand rachnā , n. f. 1. The art of poetry. 2. Writing poetry. malik-ul-shuarā ; H. kabīshuar , n. m. Prince of poets; poet laureate.
s. m. Poetry, verse. شعرخواني shiﻌr-ḵẖẉānī, s. f. Reading or studying poetry.
subs: s.f. The art of poetry; writing poetry.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شِعْر (šiʕr).

ety: prob. akin to S. शॄ
def: s.m. 'Lion of the water'; an alligator
more: n. m. 1. A tiger ( singh ). 1. Sher kā ek hī bhalā . Prov. One whelp of a lion is enough. 2. Sher kā jhūṭā gīdaṛ khāë . Prov. The jackal feeds on the leavings of the tiger. 3. Sheroṅ kā muṅh kis ne dhoyā hai? Prov. Who ever washed the tiger's mouth? (Said by the shameless). 4. Apnī galī meṅ kuttā bhī sher . Prov. A dog is a lion in his own lane. 5. Āj kal sher, bakrī ek ghāṭ pānī pīte haiṅ . Prov. Saying. Nowadays a goat and a tiger drink at the same stream. 6. Ek to sher, dūsre baktar pahne . Prov. A tiger, and clad in coat of mail! 2. ( Met .) A brave man ( bahādur , 1). 1. Asad, gar jafā par butoṅ se vafā kī, Mere sher! shābāsh, rahmat Khudā kī! Asad. If, Asad, you your mistress' cruelty with good repay, Bravo! My tiger! may God's mercy with you ever stay! 2. Zeroṅ se hī sher hote haiṅ . Prov. A tiger from a cub. (Strength from weakness grows). 3. Kartī thī bāno bayāṅ, sher-i-javāṅ ma…
adj. 1. ( sher-mard, sher-dil ) Brave ( bahādur ). 2. Overpowering; superior; stronger. 3. Excessive; too much.

s. m. A tiger, a lion. شيرآبي sher-i-ābī, s. m. An alligator. شيرقالي sher-i-ḳālī, (the effigy of a tiger) A person only fit to be looked at, a braggart. شيرگردون sher-i-gardūn, s. m. 1. The sun. 2. The sign Leo. ¶ shīr, s. m. Milk. شيرخوار shīr-ḵẖẉār or شيرخواره shīr-ḵẖẉāra, A suckling, an infant. شيرخوارگي shīr-ḵẖẉāra-gī, s. f. Sucking, living on milk. شيرخورده shīr-ḵẖụrda, A suckling. شيردار shīr-dār, adj. Milch, giving much milk. شيرشکر shīr-shakar, 1. A kind of silk-cloth. 2. Affection. شيرگرم shīr-garm, Milk-warm, lukewarm. شيرمال shīr-māl, s. f. Bread made with milk. شيرمست shīr-mast, A fine plump suckling, a fatling. شيرني shīr-i-nai, Juice of the sugar cane.
subs: s.m. A tiger's (or a lion's) cub; — a small gun
s.f. 'The lion and the kid'; a boy's game
s.m. 'The lion of God,' a title of 'Alī, and of Ḥusain
s.m. 'A tiger in effigy'; a person only fit to be looked at; a braggart
v.n. 'To utter the cry of a tiger'; to roar; to vomit with a loud noise
s.m. 'The lion of the sky,' the sun; the sign Leo
adj. & s.m. lit. 'Lion-man'; brave, valorous; — a brave man; (in the language of the devout) one who, by the practice of religious austerities, has obtained peculiar eminence in spiritual matters
v.n. 'To be the tiger'; to get the better (of), to overcome; be superior (to), be stronger; to tyrannize (over), to oppress.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شیر (šēr).

def: adj. & adv. Tiger-like; fierce as a tiger or lion; — in the manner of a tiger or lion.

ety: fr. shěʻr
def: adj. Relating to poetry, poetic, poetical.

ety: fr. sher
def: adj. Of or relating to a tiger or lion; lion-like; fierce; bold; — s.f. Lion-like conduct; fierceness; boldness, intrepidity.

ety: sher, q.v.+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. A tigress; a lioness.
more: s. f. A lioness or tigress.

def: s. Force, power ( Burh. ḳāt̤iﻌ ): (dakh.) A lion ( Harris ).
See سيش ساٴي sesh̤-sāʼī.

ety: perhaps akin to S. शिल्प
def: s.m. Business, trade, profession; manner, habit, custom, practice; amorous ways and looks (syn. ćāl; rawish; t̤arz)
more: s. m. 1. Business, trade, profession. 2. Manner, habit, custom. 3. Amorous looks. شيوه دار shewa-dār, 1. An artist, a tradesman. 2. Expert, skilful, clever.
subs: adj. & s.m. Expert, skilful, clever; — an artist, artisan; a tradesman.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شیوَه (šēwa)

def: s.m. Grief, mourning, lamentation.

ety: for A. شيعة shīʻat, v.n. fr. شيع 'to follow,' &c.
def: s.m. A particular party or sect who follow ʻAlī (affirming that he was the rightful Imām after Moḥammad); a follower of the sect of 'Alī; a sectary (opp. to sunnī).
more: n. m. شيع a sect. A follower of Ali; a sectary ( rāfzī ). Sunnī, na Shia, jī meṅ āyā so kīyā . Prov. (A free thinker). Nor Sunnī nor a Shia this, whate'er he wills comes not amiss.
(from شاع To become publick, to follow or be attached to a leader) s. m. A follower of the sect of aﻌlī, a sectary. See Saleʼs Koran, Prelim. Disc. Sect. 8.

def: adj. 1. Mixed or struck together. 2. Trembling.
s.m. A span, the space between the extremity of the thumb and that of the little finger when extended apart.
more: s. m. A span, the distance from the end of the thumb to the tip of the little finger when the hand is expanded.

ety: pl. of shadīd
def: adj. Strong, violent, vehement; — s.m. Name of an impious Eastern monarch who arrogated divine power to himself.

ety: for A. شدة, inf. n. of شد 'to be distressing,' &c.
def: s.f. Hardness, firmness; strength, power, force; vehemence, violence, intenseness, stress, pressure, severity, rigour; difficulty, hardship, adversity, trial, trouble, distress, affliction
more: n. f. شد was hard. Severity ( tezī , 4, and sakhtī 4—6). shiddat se, shadd o mad se , adv. Severely ( zorse , 3). shiddat kā, shadd o mad kā , adj. Severe ( zor-kā), Shiddat kī garmī paṛ rahī hai .
(from شدّ Heb. To plunder, lay waste) s. f. 1. Violence, force, vehemence. 2. Adversity, affliction, difficulty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شدت (šiddat), from Arabic شِدَّة (šidda).

ety: inf. n. of شفو 'to restore to health,' &c.
def: s.f. Recovery (from sickness), healing, convalescence; a medicine, remedy, cure
more: n. f. 1. Healing; cure. 2. Recovery; convalescence.
(Chald. To be quiet or tranquil) s. f. Cure, remedy, healing, recovery (from sickness), convalescence. شفا خانه shifā-ḵẖāna, s. m. An hospital.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِفَاء (šifā'), from Arabic شِفَاء (šifāʔ).

def: n. prop. Name of a Persian poet.

ety: another form of shuguft, q.v.
def: s.m. Split, rent, slit, crack, cleft, fissure, chink, crevice, chasm, flaw; — act. part. (of shigāftan), Splitting, rending, cracking, &c. (used as last member of comp.).
more: n. m. 1. Split; crack ( jhirī , 1). 2. The slit in a pen. shigāf lagānā, yā denā , v. a. To split, etc.
(properly شکاف q. v.) s. m. Split, rent, slit, cleft, chasm, fissure, crack, flaw, crevice. 2. part. act. (of شگافتن) in compos. Splitting, rending, breaking, cracking.

ety: = shagāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A jackal.
more: n. m. S. शृगाल P. شغال Heb. shââl . A jackal.

ety: cf. shikār
def: part. adj. Eating, devouring (used as last member of compounds); — s.m. Anything eatable.

ety: rt. Z. khshǒiwra = S. क्षिप्र rt. क्षिप्
def: adj. Rare, fine; great, good; of great retinue and state; powerful; venerable, respectable; glorious, beautiful, excellent; vehement, strong, vigorous; thick, stout.
more: adj. Rare, fine, great, good, of great retinue and state, powerful, beautiful, excellent, glorious, vehement, strong.

ety: or shikift; rt. Zend khshub = S. क्षुभ्
def: s.m. Wonder, astonishment.

ety: see shiguftan
def: s.m. A bud, blossom; flower; (colloq.) a fabrication; — a squib
more: n. m. 1. A bud; blossom. 2. (Mah. Wom. shafūqā ) A fabrication (اُشغلا). 3. A squib. shigūfa chhoṛnā , v. a. To let off a squib. shigūfa khilnā , v. n. To bud; bloom. shigūfa lānā , v. a. To produce something new and wonderful.
s. m. A flower, a bud. شگوفه لانا shugūfa lānā, 1. To bud, to put forth young shoots. 2. To produce something new and wonderful.
subs: To bud, blossom; to put forth young shoots; — to produce something new and wonderful.

ety: or shikufta; perf. part. of shiʻguftan, q.v.
def: part. Expanded, blown (as a flower); blooming; flourishing
more: p. p. 1. Expanded; blown (a flower). 2. Blooming. shagufta, khandah, yā hanstī peshānī , adj. Merry; jovial.

(rather شکفته shikufta, q. v.) part. pass. (of شگفتن) Expanded, blooming, blown (as a flower). شگفته خاطري shugufta-ḵẖāt̤ir-ī, s. f. Blooming or expansion of mind. شگفته هونا shugufta honā, 1. To be blown (a flower), to bloom. 2. To be delighted.
subs: s.f. Blooming or expansion of mind, delight
v.n. To be blown (a flower), to bloom; to flourish; to be delighted.

ety: abst. s. fr. shigufta
def: s.f. Expanding of a flower, blooming; bloom; beauty; flourishing state or condition; expansion (of the heart), delight, pleasure; astonishment.
more: n. f. 1. Expanding of a flower; blooming. 2. Bloom; beauty. 3. Delight; pleasure; expansion of the heart.
s. f. Expanding of a flower, blooming; delight, astonishment.

ety: orig. shikuftan, &c.; rt. Zend khshub = S. क्षुभ्
def: inf. & gerund, To blossom, bloom; blooming, &c.
more: inf. or verb. n. To bloom, blooming.

def: s.m. Neighing, neigh (of a horse).
more: s. m. Neighing of a horse.

ety: v.n. fr. شہب 'to be or become gray,' &c.
def: s.m. Flame (of fire); a firebrand; a brightly-shining star; a shooting or falling star; a meteor.
more: s. m. 1. A bright star, a falling star, a meteor. 2. Flame. 3. (Pers.) ¶ shahāb, A red colour.

def: s.m. pl. (of shajjat = shajj, q.v.), Flesh-wounds (rare). s.m. A wound or personal injury short of destroying life (rare).
s.m. = shigāf, q.v. s.m. Split, rent, slit, crack, cleft, fissure, chink, crevice, chasm, flaw; — act. part. (of shigāftan), Splitting, rending, cracking, &c. (used as last member of comp.).
s.m. The belly, stomach
more: n. m. The belly; abdomen ( peṭ 1, 2, 3). shikam-parvar ; H. peṭū ; Sant. peṭek , n. m. An epicure; a glutton. shikam-ser ; H. peṭ-bharā , adj. Full; satiated.
s. m. The belly. شکم بنده shikam-banda, An epicure, glutton.
subs: s.m. A slave to his belly, an epicure, a glutton; a servant who works for his board
or shikam-parwar, s.m. A pamperer of his belly, an epicure, a glutton; a sensualist
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَم (šikam), from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭱𐭪𐭬𐭡 (aškamb).

ety: fr. شکنب = shikam, q.v.
def: s.m. Paunch, tripe.

adj. Of or relating to the belly; big-bellied; included; private, secret; subordinate, dependent; sub-tenanted; — s.m. A big-bellied man, a glutton; — skin of the belly; — a peasant who cultivates land belonging to another; a sub-tenant
more: adj. 1. Included. Private; secret. shikmī dīvāna ; H. janam pāgal , n. m. A born idiot; a natural. kāshtkār-i-shikmī , A subtenant.

ety: rt. of shikastan; see shikast
def: part. & s.m. Breaking, crushing, overthrowing, routing; — breaker, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ʻahd-shikan, s.m. 'promise-breaker'); — s.f. Shrinking, shrivelling, constringing; — a curl; a ply, fold, plait; a furrow, crease, wrinkle, &c.
more: n. f. 1. ( In comp .) Breaking; breaker, as ahd-shikan, qilā-shikan . 2. A fold; crease; crumple; rumple ( chīn ). shikan paṛnā , v. n. To be crumpled, creased. shikan ḍālnā , v. a. To fold; crease; crumple.
s. f. 1. Shrinking, shrivelling, constringing. 2. A curl, ply, fold, furrow, wrinkle, crease, plait. 3. Part. act. (of شکستن) in compos. Breaking, breaker.
subs: (-meṅ), To be creased, be wrinkled, be crumpled, &c.

ety: abst. of shikan, q.v.
def: s.f. Breaking, breach, destruction, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ʻahd-shikanī, 'breach of agreement or promise').
more: ( In comp .) Breaking, as thāk-shikanī , Destruction of boundary marks, ahd-shikanī , Breach of agreement.

ety: fr. shikan, q.v.
def: s.m. A bookbinder's press; a clamp; stocks (for the legs); pain, torture, torment, rack
more: n. m. 1. A book-binder's press. 2. ( Mech .) A clamp. 3. Stocks (for the legs). 4. Rack; torture. shikanje meṅ kheṅchnā , v. a. 1. To put in a clamp or press. 2. To torture.
s. m. 1. Stocks (for the legs). 2. Boards in which binders press books. 3. A press. 4. Rack, torment, torture.
subs: (-ko), To put in a press; to rack, to torture.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَنْجَه (šikanja).

ety: prob. shi = Zend uz or uś = S. उद्+kār, rt. Zend kar = S. कर् (कृ, कॄ); or Zend kareṭ = S. कर्त् (कृत)
def: s.m. Hunting, the chase; prey, game; plunder, booty, pillage, spoil; — perquisites
more: n. m. 1. Hunting; booty (اﮩيرَ). Shikār ke vaqt kutyā hagāsī . Prov. When the hunter cries out tally ho, the bitch to ease herself must go. (Called to work, given to shirk). 2. Animal food; flesh; meat. Chūhā billī kā shikār hai . Prov. Rats are the natural prey of cats. (Subjects are the prey of kings). 3. A lawyer's client. shikār-band , n. m. Cords, etc. for securing game, etc. shikār k., yā mārnā , v. a. 1. To hunt; hunt down. 2. To prey upon. 3. To ensnare; entrap; bag. 4. To bring under one's clutches. shikār khelnā, shikār ko jānā , v. n. To go ahunting. Shikār ko gaë aur khud shikār ho gaë . Prov. He went to hunt, a prey became. (The biter bit). shīkār-gāh ; H. ramnā , n. f. 1. A hunting ground; a preserve for game. shikār-i-māhī , n. f. 1. Fishing. 2. A tax on fishing. shikār hāth lagnā , v. n. To bag (prey). shikār h. , v. n. To be hunted; to fall a victim. ghar meṅ shikār khel…

s. m. 1. Hunting, chase. 2. Prey, game. 3. Plunder. 4. Perquisites. شکارافگن shikār-afgan, Game-killer. شکارباز shikār-bāz, s. m. A sportsman. شکاربند shikār-band, s. m. Cords, &c. for tying game in or to (tied to a horseʼs saddle). شکارپيشه shikār-pesha, adj. Whose trade is hunting. شکارفريبي shikār-fareb-ī, s. f. Deceiving of game. شکارکرنا shikār karnā, To hunt, to catch (as prey). شکارکهيلنا shikār-khelnā, To go a hunting. شکارگاه shikār-gāh, s. f. Place for hunting in, chase.
subs: s.m. Cords, &c. for tying game in (or to fasten it to the saddle)
adj. & s.m. Whose trade or business is hunting; — a professional hunter
v.t. To hunt, take, catch as prey; to prey upon; to bring into one's clutches
(-kā), To hunt, to go a-hunting; to catch, take (fish, &c., e.g. maćhliyoṅ-kā shikār khelnā)
s.f. Hunting-ground, chase; preserve for game
To kill game; to hunt, &c. ( = shikār karnā). — shikār-ě-māhī, s.f. Fishing; — a tax on fishing
v.n. To be hunted; to fall a prey, become the prey (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَار (šikār), possibly borrowed from Sogdian 𐽁𐼸𐼰𐽀 (škʾr), from Proto-Iranian *skar-.

ety: fr. skikardan; see shikār
def: s.m. A hawk, a falcon; the sparrow-hawk.
more: n. m. The sparrow hawk. Parāë hāth par shikrā pālte ho! Prov. You train your hawk upon another's hand. (You live at another's expense).

def: adj. Of or belonging to the chase, relating to hunting; of prey; hunting-; — a hunter, sportsman, a fowler, an angler
more: adj. Belonging to the chase. shikārī jānvar , n. m. 1. Birds or beasts of prey. 2. Game birds and beasts. shikārī kuttā . A hound.
n. m. 1. A sportsman; hunter ( aheṛī ). 2. A man kept to shoot trap game.
1. adj. Of the chase, of prey, relating to hunting, &c. 2. s. m. A fowler, hunter, sportsman.
subs: s.m. A bird or beast of prey; game
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکَارِی (šikārī).

ety: v.n. fr. shikastan; rt. Zend śkeñd (śéid, śćind) = S. छिद्
def: s.f. Breaking, breakage, fracture; a breach; defeat, rout; deficiency, loss, damage; — adj. (contrac. of shikasta), Broken; odd, uneven, unpaired
more: n. f. شکستن to break. 1. A breach ( phūṭ ). 2. Breakage; fracture. 3. Loss; damage. 4. Defeat. shikast denā , v. a. To repulse; beat back; put to flight ( bhagānā , 2). shikast fāsh , n. f. Total discomfiture; complete rout. shikast khā-kar bhāgnā , v. n. To be routed. shikast o rekht ; H. ṭūṭ phūṭ, lit . breaking and falling. Injuries; damage. shikast o rekht zimma-i-mālik-i-makān . The landlord to be liable for all injuries. shikast h. , v. n. To sustain a defeat; to be worsted or discomfited.
(from شکستن) 1. s. f. Breaking, fracture, deficiency, loss, defeat. 2. (for شکسته q. v.) adj. Broken, odd, uneven, unpaired. شکست خورده shikast-ḵẖụrda, adj. Broken off, interrupted, defeated.
subs: part. adj. Broken off, interrupted; defeated
or shikast-o-reḵẖt, s.f. Injuries, damages, dilapidations; repairs (necessary for a house)
and shikast honā, v.n. To suffer defeat, to be defeated, &c.

r: shīn, śa, sha, sh, Abjad, shimāl, Shaʻbān, śa-kār
ety: = Hindī श śa; and ष sha
def: s.m. The letter or sound ś
more: is the 13th letter of Arabic, 16th of the Persian, and 19th of the Hindustānī alphabet, corresponding nearly to श in Sanskrit. Its sound is that of the English sh , of the French ch , as in cheval , and of the Italian sc , as in scimia . In abjad , q. v. it stands for 300. It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with kh , as S. shaṭ = H. khaṭ six; S. barshā = H. barkhā rain; S. rishī = H. rikhī saint; S. harash = H. harakh pleasure; S. manush =H. manukh man; dosh = dokh sin: with chh as P. & S. shash = chha six; S. shakaṭa =H. chhakṛā cart; Eng. shriek= H. chīkh ; Eng. short = H. chhoṭā (P. kotāh ); P. shīr =H. chhīr milk; bishaṭnā = bichhaṭnā to be separated; pakshi = panchhī a bird; rākshas = rāchchhas a demon; S. kaṭāksh = H. kaṭāchh a sidelong look: with ch as P. shārā = H. chīrā a turban; P. pāshīdan =P. pāchīdan sprinkle; P. hesh = P. hech insignificant: with s, q. v…

is the thirteenth letter of the Arabic and sixteenth of the Persian alphabet, corresponding nearly to श in Sanskrit. Its sound is that of the English sh; of the French ch, as in cheval; and, of the Italian sc, as in scimia. In denoting numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for 300. It is used as an abbreviation for the word شمال North; and, in dates of letters, &c. for the month شعبان. In Persian it may be used, 1. To mark the pronoun of the third person singular, in the genitive or accusative case; as, نامش His name, ثنايش His praise, گفتمش for گفتم اورا I said to him: and, it is frequently disjoined from the noun to which it has reference; as in this line of G̱ẖanīmat, زحيرتہا خرد گرديد آبش His understanding was turned to water by astonishment, for ازحيرتہا خردش آب شد. If, moreover, it relates to the same person as the agent of the sentence, it stands for the recipro…
subs: s.m. The letter or sound sha.

more: n. m. Sleight of hand; juggle.
(improp. shaﻌbada ) s. m. Sleight of hand, conjuration. سعبده باز shuﻌbada-bāz, s. m. A juggler, a conjurer. شعبده بازي shuﻌbada-bāz-ī, s. f. Conjuration, sleight of hand, juggling, hocus-pocus.
subs: s.m. One who practises legerdemain, a juggler, conjurer
s.f. Sleight of hand, conjuring, juggling trick, jugglery, hocus-pocus.

def: n. m. A profligate; a bad or loose character (بدمعاش). 1. Shohde, luchche, muë khudāī ke, Phiṭṭe-muṅh aisī be-hayāī ke! Wom. Shauq. Out on you profligate, loose wretch, godless! Accursed be of Heaven such shamelessness! 2. Mue shode ke kaun muṅh lage? Mah. Wom. Who would have anything to do with such an abandoned wretch? shohdā-pan, shohd-pan, shod-panā , n. m. Dissoluteness (بدمعاشي). shod-pan phailānā, yā k. , v. a. To play the part of a scoundrel.

n. f. shohra , n. m. Celebrity shohrat paidā k. , v. n. To acquire a name; become famous. shohrat denā , v. a. To give currency to; proclaim; blazon ( ishtehār d ). shohrat h. , v. n. See dhūm h. , 2. shohra-i-āfāq , adj. World-famous.

ety: act. part. of shoʼīdan; see sho or shū
def: part. adj. Washing (used as last member of compounds).
more: part. act. (from شوييدن) in compos. Washing.

def: adj. Bright (as colour, &c.), cheerful, gay, sprightly; buxom; brisk, spirited; humorous, sly; whimsical, capricious, playful, mischievous; petulant, saucy, bold, daring, hardened, strong; insolent, presumptuous; brazen, wanton, impudent, shameless; — s.f. A wanton
more: adj. 1. Mischievous ( burā , 7). 2. ( Poet .) Saucy; pert; playful; piquant. 3. Bright. Shokh rang . 4. Wanton; hold. shokh-chashm, yā dīdah , adj. Wanton-eyed. shokh-chashmī , n. f. Wantonness.

adj. Mischievous, playful, pert, saucy, brisk, cheerful, buxom, sly, humorous, wanton, strong, insolent, presumptuous. شوخ چشم shoḵẖ-chashm, adj. Wanton-eyed, having a playful eye, unceremonious. شوخ چشمي shoḵẖ-chashm-ī, s. f. Wantonness or playfulness of the eye.
subs: or shoḵẖ-dīda, adj. Playful-eyed; of a wanton look, lewd, impudent
s.f. Playfulness of the eye; wantonness

def: s.f. Playfulness, fun, mischief; pertness, sauciness; coquetry, wantonness; forwardness, boldness, insolence, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Mischief (شرارت). 2. Sauciness; pertness. 3. Wantonness; boldness. 1. Haṅs denā har dam nāz-bharā, dikhlānā saj dhaj shokhī kā, Har gāli misrī, qand bharī, har ek qadam aṭkhelī kā, Dil shād kyā aur moh līyā, yeh joban pāyā Holī ne! Nazir. Coquettish laughter with such saucy airs and graces shewn, Abuse as candy sweet, each step the sportive wanton's own: The heart entranced, with pleasure fed, such charms in Holi known! 2. Chhiṛaknā raṅg khūbāṅ par ajab shokhī dikhātā hai, Kabhī kuchh nāz kī 'voh voh,' kabhī andāz 'rah rah' kā . Holī. Nazīr. Pink water squirted on the fair. such pretty airs they show, Now such coquettish ' oh, oh, ohs!' now pretty ' hold, hold ,' do! shokhī se , adv. 1. Saucily. 2. Wantonly. shokhī k. , v. a. To be naughty, saucy.
s. f. Mischief, playfulness, fun, pertness, forwardness, humour, sauciness, wantonness, coquetry.
subs: adv. Playfully, in mischief; pertly; wantonly, &c.
To practise mischief, &c.; to be pert or saucy; to be insolent; to be wanton, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شوخِی (šōxī).

ety: for A. شعلة shǒʻlat, v.n. fr. شعل 'to burn, blaze,' &c.
def: s.m. Flame, blaze, light, flash
more: n. m. A flame. shola-khū , Poet. adj. Hot; fiery. shola-rū , Poet. n. m. A blazing beauty. sholā bhabūkā h. , v. n. To be inflamed; to burn with rage.

(from شعل To kindle fire) s. m. Flame, blaze, light, flash. شعله افشان shuﻌla-afshān or شعله فشان shuﻌla-fishān, Scattering flame, flame-scatterer. شعله بار shuﻌla-bār, adj. Raining flames. شعله پيشه shuﻌla-pesha, adj. Flame in practice, burning as a flame. شعله خو shuﻌla-ḵẖū, adj. Fiery in disposition. شعله خوئي shuﻌla-ḵẖū-ī, s. f. Fieriness of disposition. شعله خيز shuﻌla-ḵẖez, adj. Flame-exciting. شعله خيزي shuﻌla-ḵẖez-ī, s. f. The rising up or exciting of flame. شعله رخ shuﻌla-ruḵẖ, or شعله رو shuﻌla-rū, adj. Flame-faced, having a bright countenance. شعله زن shuﻌla-zan, adj. Blazing, flashing, brilliant.
subs: adj. & s.m. Dispersing or scattering flame; — flame-scatterer
adj. & s.m. Constantly setting aflame or ablaze; burning as a flame; — one whose regular business it is to set aflame or ablaze
adj. Fiery in disposition, fiery, hot
adj. Raising a flame, flame-exciting, making a blaze
or shǒʻla-rū, adj. & s.m. Flame-faced, having a bright countenance; a blazing beauty
adj. Blazing, flashing, flaming, brilliant, resplendent
or shǒʻla-bhabūkā honā, v.n. To be inflamed, to burn with rage.
ex: یہ سچ کہ بھڑکتے شعلوں سے دامن کو بچانا کھیل نہیں
It is true that to [try and] save a soul from a blazing fire is no easy task
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شُعْلَه (šu'la), borrowed from Arabic شُعْلَة (šuʕla). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi شعلہ (ś'lh).

def: s.m. corr. of shora, q.v. adj. & s.m. Barren (ground); marshy; — barren or nitrous soil; — saltpetre; nitre; nitrate of potash
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of saltpetre, made of saltpetre

def: s.m. A dish of rice and pulse boiled together till quite soft; soft khićṛī, q.v.
more: n. m. Cor. of shulla liquid. A dish of rice and pulse boiled soft together and given to sick persons; soft khichṛī .
(corrupt. from شله shula ) s. m. A dish consisting of rice and pulse boiled together and given to sick persons.
s. m. A dish, khichṛī with meat ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: prob. shūm, q.v.+Prk. आलिओ, and आलिआ = S. आल+इकः, and इका
def: adj. (dialec.) Slothful, lazy, indolent ( = sust); — s.f. Sloth, indolence ( = sustī).
more: (dakh) 1. adj. Slothful, lazy, indolent. 2. s. f. Sloth, indolence: see سستي sust-ī.

ety: rt. Zend khshar = S. क्षर्
def: s.m. Cry, noise, outcry, exclamation, din, clamour, uproar, tumult, disturbance; renown; — adj. Disturbed (in mind), mad ( = shorīda); — salt, brackish (cf. S. kshāra); very bitter; — unlucky
more: n. m. Cry; outcry; din; clamour ( dhūm , 1). shor k., yā machānā , v. a. To make a noise, uproar, row.

n. m. A saline substance; anatron. shor , adj. Salt; brackish. shor zamīn ; H. kallhaṛ , E. usal , n. f. Land impregnated with saltpetre; saline soil. Dekho, unkī chīz na chheṛo! uṭheṅge to shor kareṅge. shor, yā khār lagnā , v. n. To become saline.

1. s. m. Cry, noise, outcry, clamour, din, renown, uproar, exclamation, disturbance, tumult. 2. adj. Salt, brackish, very bitter. 3. adj. Unlucky. 4. part. act. (of شوريدن) Disturbed, mad. شورانگيز shor-angez, Exciting tumults. شورآور shor-āwar, adj. Tumultuous, noisy, calamitous. شوربخت shor-baḵẖt, adj. Unfortunate, wretched, infamous. شوربور shor-bor (see شرابور) Very wet, drenched. شورپشت shor-pusht, adj. Quarrelsome, encouraging disturbance, noisy. شورچشم shor-chashm, adj. Having an unlucky or malignant eye. شورزمين shor-zamīn, s. f. Salsuginous ground, barren land. شورشار shor-shār or شورشر shor-sharr, or شورشرابا shor-sharābā, or شورشغب shor-shag̠ẖab, s. m. Noise, tumult, disturbance, bustle, clamour. شورمچانا shor-machānā, To make a noise, to hoot.
subs: (or shora-pusht), adj. Noisy, quarrelsome, encouraging disturbance, refractory, unruly
adj. Having an unlucky or malignant eye
or shor-sharābā, or shor-shag̠ab, s.m. Noise, bustle, clamour, tumult, disturbance
v.n. To make a noise, create an uproar or disturbance, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شور (šōr).

ety: fr. shor, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Barren (ground); marshy; — barren or nitrous soil; — saltpetre; nitre; nitrate of potash
more: 1. adj. Marshy, barren (ground). 2. s. m. Nitre, saltpetre. شوره گر shora-gar, s. m. A manufacturer of saltpetre.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of saltpetre, made of saltpetre

def: n. f. Refractoriness; turbulence.
s.m. Soup, broth.
more: n. m. Broth; soup. Ginī boṭī (ḍalī), napā shurvā . Prov. Counted each bit of meat with measured helps of soup. (Rigid economy).
wety: From Classical Persian شوربا (šōrbā).

def: s. (dakh.) Broth: see شوربا shorbā.
adj. = sharābor, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of shorīdan, fr. shor, q.v.
def: part. adj. Disturbed (in mind), distracted, mad, frantic; desperately in love; faint; dejected
more: part. pass. (of شوريدن) 1. Disturbed, mad, desperately in love. 2. Faint, dejected. شوريده حال shorīda-ḥāl, adj. Mad, &c. شوريده خاطر shorīda-ḵẖāt̤ir, adj. Dejected, melancholy.
subs: adj. In a distracted state (of mind), demented, mad, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شوریده (šōrīda).

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Derangement, distractedness, madness (from love); love, insane passion.

ety: abst. s. fr. shor, q.v.
def: s.f. Commotion, confusion, tumult, disturbance, insurrection, &c.; — brackishness, saltness
more: n. f. Disturbance, etc. ( balvā ). shorish barpā k. , v. a. To excite an insurrection.
(from شوريدن) s. f. 1. Tumult, insurrection, confusion. 2. Brackishness, saltness.
subs: To create a disturbance, excite an insurrection.

ety: shor, q.v.+A. abst. aff. -īyat
def: s.f. Saline quality; brackishness, saltness.
s.m. = shorbā, q.v. s.m. Soup, broth.
more: s. (dakh.) Broth ( Harris ): see شوربا shorbā.

ety: cf. شعشعه shǒʻshaʻa
def: s.m. An ingot (of gold or silver); a bar (of iron, &c.); a chip; a particle, part, bit, piece; rubbish, filth.
more: s. m. 1. An ingot. 2. Chips, rubbish, filth. 3. A particle, a part.

ety: for A. شعشعة, inf. n. of شعشع 'to make thin and long'
def: s.m. A long thin stroke, a stroke; tail of a letter); a cedilla; — fiction, hoax, false report
more: n. m. 1. Fiction ( ushgula ). 2. A stroke. He kā shoshā . 3. A cedilla. shoshā uṭhānā, yā chhoṛnā , v. a. To spread a false report. Hazrat ne āte hī nayā shoshā chhoṛā . No sooner come His Highness lies invents. shoshā denā , v. a. 1. To make a stroke. 2. To excite (اُکسانا 6.).
subs: or shoshā ćhoṛnā, v.n. To spread a false report, &c.
(-ko), To make a stroke (to); to excite.

def: n. m. Funeral obsequies. It consists chiefly in offering cakes ( pinḍa ), water, etc. to deceased ancestors before a sacrificial fire.
s.m. pl. (of shāʻir), Poets.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شعرا (šu'arā), from Arabic شُعَرَاء (šuʕarāʔ).

def: s.m. corr. of shubha, q.v. s.m. Confusedness, dubiousness, doubt, uncertainty, ambiguity; hesitation, suspicion; suspense; question, scruple; a defect (esp. in law), a flaw; presumption (of guilt)
subs: (-kā), To entertain doubt (of), to doubt; to suspect, distrust
wety: From Sanskrit शुभ (śubha).

ety: i.q. ćupān or ćūpān, q.v.
def: s.m. A shepherd, pastor.

ety: for A. شبہة shubhat, v.n. fr. شبه; ii, شبّه 'to render confused or dubious,' &c.
def: s.m. Confusedness, dubiousness, doubt, uncertainty, ambiguity; hesitation, suspicion; suspense; question, scruple; a defect (esp. in law), a flaw; presumption (of guilt)
more: (from شبه To liken, to compare, dissemble, render uncertain) s. m. Doubt, suspicion, ambiguity, hesitation.
subs: (-kā), To entertain doubt (of), to doubt; to suspect, distrust
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شبهه (šubha), from Arabic شُبْهَة (šubha), from verb شَبَّهَ (šabbaha, “to render confused”).

def: adv. So so.

ety: perf. part. of shudan; and = Zend shūta; S. च्युत
def: part. Become, having become; past, gone; lost
more: part. pass. (of شدن) Become, having become, gone, lost.
subs: adv. Going on (from one thing to another) gradually; by degrees, gradually (syn. hote hote).

def: s. (dakh.) See شـﮩدا shuhdā.
adv. By degrees.

ety: v.n. of shudan, and āmadan
def: s.f. A going and coming; custom, usage.

ety: rt. Zend shu = S. च्यु; — dan = S. aff. तं
def: inf. & gerund, To become; becoming (used in comp.).
more: inf. or verbal noun, in compos. To become, becoming.
